title: Structured controls for the manual interview and priorities
userStory: As a user following the manual setup path, I want structured input controls (comboboxes, chip toggles, selects, and structured rows) instead of free-text fields, so that I can enter my context quickly and consistently without guessing the expected format.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Phase 4 manual path Role field renders a combobox (shadcn Command/Popover pattern) pre-seeded with at least 8 common titles (e.g. 'Product Manager', 'Product Operations Lead', 'Program Manager', 'Chief of Staff', 'Operations Manager', 'Head of Product', 'Director of Product', 'Delivery Manager'). Typing a value not in the list and pressing Enter or selecting a 'Create' option accepts it as a free-entry value. The selected value persists to `onboarding-state.json` under `answers.role`. `grep -n 'Command\|Combobox\|RoleCombobox' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match inside the `PhaseFourManual` block.

Working style field renders at least 6 pre-defined chip options (e.g. 'async-first', 'deep-work mornings', 'meeting-heavy', 'batched afternoons', 'structured days', 'flex schedule'). Clicking a chip toggles its selected state (selected chips render with a distinct filled/highlighted visual treatment). A free-entry input adds custom chips. Selected values persist to `onboarding-state.json` under `answers.workstyle`. `grep -n 'workstyle\|StyleChip\|workStyle' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns a match in the chip-selector block.

Team field renders a size Select with exactly four options ('1–2', '3–5', '6–10', '11+') plus a free-text descriptor input below it. Both values persist to `onboarding-state.json` under `answers.team` as an object `{ size: string; descriptor: string }`. `normalizeAnswers` migrates the old flat `team: string` shape by placing the string into `descriptor` and leaving `size` empty. `cat docs/data/local/onboarding-state.json` after saving phase 4 shows `answers.team` as an object with both keys.

Stakeholders field renders structured rows, each with: Name (text input), Role/Title (text input), and Relationship (Select with exactly three options: 'Decision maker', 'Collaborator', 'Informed'). An 'Add stakeholder' button appends a new empty row; a Remove (×) button deletes a row. Values persist to `onboarding-state.json` under `answers.stakeholders` as an array of `{ name: string; role: string; relationship: string }` objects. `normalizeAnswers` migrates old `stakeholders: string[]` by treating each string as `{ name: string, role: '', relationship: '' }`. `grep -n 'StakeholderRow\|stakeholderRows\|relationship' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match.

Phase 5 priority owner field renders a Select pre-populated with 'Me' plus each stakeholder name from `answers.stakeholders`, plus a free-entry fallback option. Verify: complete phase 4 with stakeholders named 'Alice Chen' and 'Bob Park', navigate to phase 5 — the owner Select for each priority shows at minimum 'Me', 'Alice Chen', 'Bob Park'. `grep -n 'ownerOptions\|stakeholderNames\|priorityOwner' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match.

`npx tsc --noEmit` in `work-hq/` returns no type errors after this story. After completing phases 4–5 with the new structured controls, the `scaffold-files` server function still produces valid output: `cat context/me.md` shows real content with no `[bracketed]` placeholders, and `cat context/active.md` shows the three priorities each with a title, owner, and due date.

technicalNotes: Edit `PhaseFourManual` in `work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` (story 003). shadcn/ui is already configured (`work-hq/components.json`); use its `Select`, `Command`, `Popover`, and `Badge` primitives per the existing design system. Role: shadcn `Command`-based combobox in a `Popover`. Workstyle: custom chip-toggle component using `Badge` primitives; `answers.workstyle` shape (currently `string`) may remain a CSV string for backward compat or be migrated to `string[]` — define the shape in `normalizeAnswers` explicitly. Key tools: same combobox pattern as Role, pre-seeded with Jira, Confluence, Slack, Glean, Notion, Figma, GitHub, Linear, Asana plus free-entry; persists to `answers.keyTools: string[]` (already an array, no migration required). Stakeholders: replace `stakeholders: string[]` with `Array<{ name: string; role: string; relationship: string }>`. Team: replace `team: string` with `{ size: string; descriptor: string }`. Both shape changes require entries in `normalizeAnswers` (line ~94). Phase 5 `PhaseNow`: derive `ownerOptions` from `answers.stakeholders.map(s => s.name)` prepended with 'Me'. All controls must retain a free-text escape hatch per the butler principle (os-helper SKILL.md).
outOfScope: The ActionCard component (story 012). Phase 4 seeded-path per-fact triage (story 013). Rewriting the `scaffold-files` server function — only the data shape it receives must remain valid. Phase 1, 2, and 3 controls.

verificationPlan.command: cat docs/data/local/onboarding-state.json | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('team:', d['answers']['team']); print('stakeholders:', d['answers']['stakeholders'])"
verificationPlan.checks: team is an object with keys size and descriptor; stakeholders is an array of objects each with keys name, role, relationship.
Exits with code 0 and no output — no TypeScript type errors introduced.
Returns at least one match confirming structured stakeholder rows are implemented.

impl.notes: Replaced the phase-4 manual-path free-text controls and the phase-5 owner input with structured shadcn-primitive controls, each retaining a free-entry escape hatch (butler principle). New route-local components in onboarding.tsx: (1) RoleCombobox — single-value combobox = shadcn Command inside a Popover, seeded with 8 titles (ROLE_OPTIONS: Product Manager, Product Operations Lead, Program Manager, Chief of Staff, Operations Manager, Head of Product, Director of Product, Delivery Manager); typing a non-listed value surfaces a 'Create "x"' CommandItem that cmdk auto-highlights so Enter accepts free entry (AC1). (2) WorkstyleChips — toggleable chips styled via the design-system badgeVariants cva (default variant when selected, outline when not) as real <button aria-pressed> controls, seeded with 6 options (WORKSTYLE_OPTIONS), plus a free-entry input (Enter or Add) for custom chips; persists to answers.workstyle as string[] (AC2). (3) TeamControl — a shadcn Select with exactly four buckets (TEAM_SIZES 1–2/3–5/6–10/11+) rendered '<n> people' + a free-text descriptor input; persists answers.team as { size, descriptor } (AC3). (4) KeyToolsCombobox — multi-select Command/Popover seeded with common enterprise tools (GitHub Copilot, Jira, Confluence, Slack, Glean, Notion, Figma, GitHub, Linear, Asana) + a 'Add "x"' free-entry row; selected tools render as removable secondary Badge chips; stays answers.keyTools: string[] (no migration). (5) StakeholderRows — structured rows each with Name input, Role/Title input, and a Relationship Select (RELATIONSHIPS: Decision maker/Collaborator/Informed); 'Add stakeholder' appends an empty row, per-row × removes; persists answers.stakeholders as Array<{ name, role, relationship }> (AC4). (6) OwnerSelect (phase 5) — a Select whose options are ownerOptions = ['Me', ...stakeholderNames] (stakeholderNames derived from answers.stakeholders[].name, de-duped) plus a 'Someone else…' sentinel that reveals a free-entry input; an owner value not in the list opens in custom mode automatically (AC5). Answers type extended: workstyle string→string[], team string→{size,descriptor} (Team type), stakeholders string[]→Stakeholder[]; emptyAnswers updated. normalizeAnswers now runs normalizeWorkstyle (array kept minus empties; legacy CSV string split on commas into chips — lossless on rejoin; single string→one chip), normalizeTeam (object coerced key-by-key; legacy string→{size:'',descriptor:string}), normalizeStakeholders (objects coerced; legacy string[]→name-only rows), with the migrated keys placed AFTER the raw spread so legacy shapes always upgrade. Review-step summaries render structured data legibly via teamSummary (descriptor — <size> people) and stakeholderSummary ('Name (Role) — relationship'); workstyle joined comma-wise. scaffold-files.ts: answersSchema widened with unions (workstyle string|string[], team string|{size,descriptor}, stakeholders array of string|{name,role,relationship}) so the server stays valid for either shape; added coercion helpers workstyleToLine/teamToLine/stakeholderLines; buildMeContent + buildOrgContent now emit the coerced one-line/bullet forms — me.md '**Working style:**' one comma line, '**Team:**' descriptor + '<size> people'; org.md '## Team structure' descriptor line and '## Key stakeholders' bullets 'Name (Role) — relationship' with name-less rows dropped. Output uses parentheses only (never '['), preserving the file-contracts §1.1/§1.2 headings and the non-placeholder heuristic. Token-only styling (primary/border/card/muted/foreground/ring/destructive/secondary — no new tokens); shadcn primitives used via their existing wrappers (Command/Popover/Select/Badge) — no hand-rolled popovers. WCAG 2.2 AA: every control labelled (Field group labels + per-input <label htmlFor> in TeamControl/StakeholderRows, aria-label on combobox triggers via visible text and on remove/add buttons), combobox keyboard-operable with Check aria on selected items, chips aria-pressed, shared FOCUS_RING focus-visible rings, prefers-reduced-motion respected (no transition-all). No /style-guide route exists in this project (Mode A never run) and the new controls are route-local onboarding composites, not extracted design-system components, so no style-guide update applies. No backend/schema/migration work (scaffold-files change is answer-mapping coercion only, not new server logic).

verificationRun.result: PASS — client + server build ✓ (onboarding client chunk 127.25 kB gzip 29.21 kB); re-checked clean after prettier --write.
PASS — both files match Prettier code style (onboarding.tsx formatted with --write, then re-checked).

PASS — RoleCombobox defined + used inside PhaseFourManual About step; WorkstyleChips in the chip block; StakeholderRows + relationship field present; ownerOptions/stakeholderNames derived and passed to OwnerSelect in PhaseNow.

verificationRun.command: node scratchpad/verify-014.mjs — logic-mirror of scaffold-files coercion/builders + onboarding normalize* migrations
verificationRun.result: PASS — 20/20 assertions. Structured answers → me/org/active with NO '[' placeholders; workstyle→one comma line; team→'descriptor — <size> people'; stakeholders→'Name (Role) — relationship' with name-less rows dropped; priority line format intact. Legacy migrations lossless: workstyle CSV→3 chips, team string→descriptor(empty size), stakeholders string[]→name-only rows, ['']→one empty row; migrated legacy answers still build markdown with no '['.

RUNTIME — the object/array shapes are written after a browser edit in phase 4; no headless-browser harness available in this environment. The persisted shapes are proven by the tsc types, normalizeAnswers migration, and the verify-014.mjs mirror; the on-disk file currently holds pre-story-014 flat shapes used to exercise the migration path on load.
